steps.title: Spot the cause-and-effect link
steps.content: The first sentence establishes what Greenidge-Sabral wanted to avoid—harsh synthetic dyes. The second sentence reveals what she actually did—experimented with natural dyes. The second action is the direct result of the first desire.

steps.title: Choose the transition that reflects the causal flow
steps.content:
”With this in mind” means “considering this goal” and correctly connects her desire to avoid harsh dyes with her action to experiment with natural methods.

The other choices either incorrectly summarize prior ideas (“To sum up”) or falsely signal contradiction when her action aligns with her stated goal (“In actuality,” “Nevertheless”).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
